revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun | name = {{ill|Tsuruyama Kofun|ja|鶴山古墳}} | image = [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus mound (front part on the right, rear part on the left) | alternative_name = Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun | location = {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|19|27.08|N|139|20|25.88|E|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} (Keyhole-shaped tomb) | size = Mound length: 95 m
Height: 7.5 m (rear circular part) | burial = {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (Vertical pit stone chamber, wooden coffin inside) | artifacts = Human remains, armor, helmets, numerous grave goods | construction_period = Mid-5th century | designated_as = {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|ja|群馬県指定史跡}} (1951) }} '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' (つるやまこふん) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}, or keyhole-shaped tumulus, and has been designated as a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == Tsuruyama Kofun is a '''large keyhole-shaped tumulus''' built on the southeastern edge of the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地}}, west of the {{ill|Kanayama Hills|ja|金山丘陵}} in eastern Gunma Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage, especially to its front section, and an excavation survey was conducted in '''1948'''. The mound does '''not''' feature a stone covering ({{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) or '''hollow clay figures''' ({{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}). A moat encircles the mound. The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. Additionally, a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}) was found to the north of the stone chamber. Excavations revealed '''human remains''', '''two helmets''', '''three cuirasses''', and numerous '''iron and stone objects'''. The tumulus is estimated to date to the '''mid-5th century''', within the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is considered significant in understanding the '''political landscape of eastern Gunma''', particularly in relation to the nearby '''Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun''', the largest kofun in eastern Japan. In '''1951''', the tumulus was designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's dimensions are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' * Diameter: ~50 meters * Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' * Width: ~55 meters * Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == Burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber dimensions: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == Artifacts == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifacts from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs * Sickle ×5 * Axe ×5 * Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs * Sickle ×3 * Axe ×2 * Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' * '''Horned helmet''' ×1 * '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|Type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were performed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a formal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Cultural Heritage == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館}} – Displays artifacts from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} * [[Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto)|Horned helmet]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuruyama Kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] The chamber dimensions: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == Artifacts == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifacts from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs * Sickle ×5 * Axe ×5 * Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs * Sickle ×3 * Axe ×2 * Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' * '''Horned helmet''' ×1 * '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|Type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were performed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a formal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Cultural Heritage == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館}} – Displays artifacts from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} * [[Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto)|Horned helmet]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuruyama Kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun | name = {{ill|Tsuruyama Kofun|ja|鶴山古墳}} | image = [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus mound (front part on the right, rear part on the left) | alternative_name = Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun | location = {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|19|27.08|N|139|20|25.88|E|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} (Keyhole-shaped tomb) | size = Mound length: 95 m
Height: 7.5 m (rear circular part) | burial = {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (Vertical pit stone chamber, wooden coffin inside) | artifacts = Human remains, armor, helmets, numerous grave goods | construction_period = Mid-5th century | designated_as = {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|ja|群馬県指定史跡}} (1951) }} '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' (つるやまこふん) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}, or keyhole-shaped tumulus, and has been designated as a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == Tsuruyama Kofun is a '''large keyhole-shaped tumulus''' built on the southeastern edge of the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地}}, west of the {{ill|Kanayama Hills|ja|金山丘陵}} in eastern Gunma Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage, especially to its front section, and an excavation survey was conducted in '''1948'''. The mound does '''not''' feature a stone covering ({{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) or '''hollow clay figures''' ({{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}). A moat encircles the mound. The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. Additionally, a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}) was found to the north of the stone chamber. Excavations revealed '''human remains''', '''two helmets''', '''three cuirasses''', and numerous '''iron and stone objects'''. The tumulus is estimated to date to the '''mid-5th century''', within the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is considered significant in understanding the '''political landscape of eastern Gunma''', particularly in relation to the nearby '''Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun''', the largest kofun in eastern Japan. In '''1951''', the tumulus was designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's dimensions are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' * Diameter: ~50 meters * Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' * Width: ~55 meters * Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == Burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber dimensions: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == Artifacts == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifacts from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs * Sickle ×5 * Axe ×5 * Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs * Sickle ×3 * Axe ×2 * Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' * '''Horned helmet''' ×1 * '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|Type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were performed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a formal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Cultural Heritage == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館}} – Displays artifacts from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} * [[Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto)|Horned helmet]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuruyama Kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] {{Infobox Kofun | name = {{ill|Tsuruyama Kofun|ja|鶴山古墳}} | image = [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus mound (front part on the right, rear part on the left) | alternative_name = Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun | location = {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|19|27.08|N|139|20|25.88|E|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} (Keyhole-shaped tomb) | size = Mound length: 95 m
Height: 7.5 m (rear circular part) | burial = {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (Vertical pit stone chamber, wooden coffin inside) | artifacts = Human remains, armor, helmets, numerous grave goods | construction_period = Mid-5th century | designated_as = {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|ja|群馬県指定史跡}} (1951) }} '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' (つるやまこふん) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}, or keyhole-shaped tumulus, and has been designated as a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == Tsuruyama Kofun is a '''large keyhole-shaped tumulus''' built on the southeastern edge of the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地}}, west of the {{ill|Kanayama Hills|ja|金山丘陵}} in eastern Gunma Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage, especially to its front section, and an excavation survey was conducted in '''1948'''. The mound does '''not''' feature a stone covering ({{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) or '''hollow clay figures''' ({{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}). A moat encircles the mound. The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. Additionally, a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}) was found to the north of the stone chamber. Excavations revealed '''human remains''', '''two helmets''', '''three cuirasses''', and numerous '''iron and stone objects'''. The tumulus is estimated to date to the '''mid-5th century''', within the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is considered significant in understanding the '''political landscape of eastern Gunma''', particularly in relation to the nearby '''Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun''', the largest kofun in eastern Japan. In '''1951''', the tumulus was designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's dimensions are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' * Diameter: ~50 meters * Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' * Width: ~55 meters * Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == Burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber dimensions: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == Artifacts == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifacts from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs * Sickle ×5 * Axe ×5 * Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs * Sickle ×3 * Axe ×2 * Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' * '''Horned helmet''' ×1 * '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|Type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were performed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a formal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Cultural Heritage == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館}} – Displays artifacts from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} * [[Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto)|Horned helmet]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuruyama Kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] It is classified as a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}, or keyhole-shaped tumulus, and has been designated as a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == Tsuruyama Kofun is a '''large keyhole-shaped tumulus''' built on the southeastern edge of the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地}}, west of the {{ill|Kanayama Hills|ja|金山丘陵}} in eastern Gunma Prefecture. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were performed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a formal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Cultural Heritage == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun | name = {{ill|Tsuruyama Kofun|ja|鶴山古墳}} | image = [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus mound (front part on the right, rear part on the left) | alternative_name = Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun | location = {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|19|27.08|N|139|20|25.88|E|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} (Keyhole-shaped tomb) | size = Mound length: 95 m
Height: 7.5 m (rear circular part) | burial = {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (Vertical pit stone chamber, wooden coffin inside) | artifacts = Human remains, armor, helmets, numerous grave goods | construction_period = Mid-5th century | designated_as = {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|ja|群馬県指定史跡}} (1951) }} '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' (つるやまこふん) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}, or keyhole-shaped tumulus, and has been designated as a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == Tsuruyama Kofun is a '''large keyhole-shaped tumulus''' built on the southeastern edge of the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地}}, west of the {{ill|Kanayama Hills|ja|金山丘陵}} in eastern Gunma Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage, especially to its front section, and an excavation survey was conducted in '''1948'''. The mound does '''not''' feature a stone covering ({{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) or '''hollow clay figures''' ({{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}). A moat encircles the mound. The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. Additionally, a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}) was found to the north of the stone chamber. Excavations revealed '''human remains''', '''two helmets''', '''three cuirasses''', and numerous '''iron and stone objects'''. The tumulus is estimated to date to the '''mid-5th century''', within the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is considered significant in understanding the '''political landscape of eastern Gunma''', particularly in relation to the nearby '''Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun''', the largest kofun in eastern Japan. In '''1951''', the tumulus was designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's dimensions are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' * Diameter: ~50 meters * Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' * Width: ~55 meters * Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == Burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber dimensions: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == Artifacts == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifacts from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs * Sickle ×5 * Axe ×5 * Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs * Sickle ×3 * Axe ×2 * Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' * '''Horned helmet''' ×1 * '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|Type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were performed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a formal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Cultural Heritage == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館}} – Displays artifacts from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} * [[Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto)|Horned helmet]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuruyama Kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] {{Infobox Kofun | name = {{ill|Tsuruyama Kofun|ja|鶴山古墳}} | image = [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus mound (front part on the right, rear part on the left) | alternative_name = Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun | location = {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|19|27.08|N|139|20|25.88|E|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Keyhole-shaped tomb) | size = Mound length: 95 m
Height: 7.5 m (rear circular part) | burial = {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (Vertical pit stone chamber, wooden coffin inside) | artifacts = Human remains, armor, helmets, numerous grave goods | construction_period = Mid-5th century | designated_as = {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|ja|群馬県指定史跡}} (1951) }} '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' (つるやまこふん) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, or keyhole-shaped tumulus, and has been designated as a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == Tsuruyama Kofun is a '''large keyhole-shaped tumulus''' built on the southeastern edge of the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地}}, west of the {{ill|Kanayama Hills|ja|金山丘陵}} in eastern Gunma Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage, especially to its front section, and an excavation survey was conducted in '''1948'''. The mound does '''not''' feature a stone covering ({{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) or '''hollow clay figures''' ({{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}). A moat encircles the mound. The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. Additionally, a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}) was found to the north of the stone chamber. Excavations revealed '''human remains''', '''two helmets''', '''three cuirasses''', and numerous '''iron and stone objects'''. The tumulus is estimated to date to the '''mid-5th century''', within the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is considered significant in understanding the '''political landscape of eastern Gunma''', particularly in relation to the nearby '''Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun''', the largest kofun in eastern Japan. In '''1951''', the tumulus was designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's dimensions are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' * Diameter: ~50 meters * Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' * Width: ~55 meters * Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == Burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber dimensions: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == Artifacts == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifacts from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs * Sickle ×5 * Axe ×5 * Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs * Sickle ×3 * Axe ×2 * Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' * '''Horned helmet''' ×1 * '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were performed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a formal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Cultural Heritage == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館}} – Displays artifacts from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} * [[Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto)|Horned helmet]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuruyama Kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] Tumulus mound (front part on the right, rear part on the left) | alternative_name = Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun | location = {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|19|27.08|N|139|20|25.88|E|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Keyhole-shaped tomb) | size = Mound length: 95 m
Height: 7.5 m (rear circular part) | burial = {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (Vertical pit stone chamber, wooden coffin inside) | artifacts = Human remains, armor, helmets, numerous grave goods | construction_period = Mid-5th century | designated_as = {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|ja|群馬県指定史跡}} (1951) }} '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' (つるやまこふん) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, or keyhole-shaped tumulus, and has been designated as a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == Tsuruyama Kofun is a '''large keyhole-shaped tumulus''' built on the southeastern edge of the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地}}, west of the {{ill|Kanayama Hills|ja|金山丘陵}} in eastern Gunma Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage, especially to its front section, and an excavation survey was conducted in '''1948'''. The mound does '''not''' feature a stone covering ({{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) or '''hollow clay figures''' ({{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}). A moat encircles the mound. The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. Additionally, a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}) was found to the north of the stone chamber. Excavations revealed '''human remains''', '''two helmets''', '''three cuirasses''', and numerous '''iron and stone objects'''. The tumulus is estimated to date to the '''mid-5th century''', within the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is considered significant in understanding the '''political landscape of eastern Gunma''', particularly in relation to the nearby '''Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun''', the largest kofun in eastern Japan. In '''1951''', the tumulus was designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's dimensions are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' * Diameter: ~50 meters * Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' * Width: ~55 meters * Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == Burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber dimensions: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == Artifacts == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifacts from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs * Sickle ×5 * Axe ×5 * Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs * Sickle ×3 * Axe ×2 * Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' * '''Horned helmet''' ×1 * '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were performed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a formal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Cultural Heritage == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館}} – Displays artifacts from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} * [[Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto)|Horned helmet]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuruyama Kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun | name = {{ill|Tsuruyama Kofun|ja|鶴山古墳}} | image = [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus mound (front part on the right, rear part on the left) | alternative_name = Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun | location = {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|19|27.08|N|139|20|25.88|E|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Keyhole-shaped tomb) | size = Mound length: 95 m
Height: 7.5 m (rear circular part) | burial = {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (Vertical pit stone chamber, wooden coffin inside) | artifacts = Human remains, armor, helmets, numerous grave goods | construction_period = Mid-5th century | designated_as = {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|ja|群馬県指定史跡}} (1951) }} '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' (つるやまこふん) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, or keyhole-shaped tumulus, and has been designated as a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == Tsuruyama Kofun is a '''large keyhole-shaped tumulus''' built on the southeastern edge of the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地}}, west of the {{ill|Kanayama Hills|ja|金山丘陵}} in eastern Gunma Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage, especially to its front section, and an excavation survey was conducted in '''1948'''. The mound does '''not''' feature a stone covering ({{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) or '''hollow clay figures''' ({{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}). A moat encircles the mound. The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. Additionally, a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}) was found to the north of the stone chamber. Excavations revealed '''human remains''', '''two helmets''', '''three cuirasses''', and numerous '''iron and stone objects'''. The tumulus is estimated to date to the '''mid-5th century''', within the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is considered significant in understanding the '''political landscape of eastern Gunma''', particularly in relation to the nearby '''Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun''', the largest kofun in eastern Japan. In '''1951''', the tumulus was designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's dimensions are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' * Diameter: ~50 meters * Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' * Width: ~55 meters * Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == Burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber dimensions: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == Artifacts == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifacts from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs * Sickle ×5 * Axe ×5 * Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs * Sickle ×3 * Axe ×2 * Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' * '''Horned helmet''' ×1 * '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were performed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a formal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Cultural Heritage == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館}} – Displays artifacts from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} * [[Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto)|Horned helmet]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuruyama Kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] revision.comment: Text replacement - "上毛野氏}}" to "上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}"
revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun | name = {{ill|Tsuruyama Kofun|ja|鶴山古墳}} | image = [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus mound (front part on the right, rear part on the left) | alternative_name = Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun | location = {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|19|27.08|N|139|20|25.88|E|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Keyhole-shaped tomb) | size = Mound length: 95 m
Height: 7.5 m (rear circular part) | burial = {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (Vertical pit stone chamber, wooden coffin inside) | artifacts = Human remains, armor, helmets, numerous grave goods | construction_period = Mid-5th century | designated_as = {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|ja|群馬県指定史跡}} (1951) }} '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' (つるやまこふん) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, or keyhole-shaped tumulus, and has been designated as a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == Tsuruyama Kofun is a '''large keyhole-shaped tumulus''' built on the southeastern edge of the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地}}, west of the {{ill|Kanayama Hills|ja|金山丘陵}} in eastern Gunma Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage, especially to its front section, and an excavation survey was conducted in '''1948'''. The mound does '''not''' feature a stone covering ({{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) or '''hollow clay figures''' ({{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}). A moat encircles the mound. The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. Additionally, a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}) was found to the north of the stone chamber. Excavations revealed '''human remains''', '''two helmets''', '''three cuirasses''', and numerous '''iron and stone objects'''. The tumulus is estimated to date to the '''mid-5th century''', within the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is considered significant in understanding the '''political landscape of eastern Gunma''', particularly in relation to the nearby '''Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun''', the largest kofun in eastern Japan. In '''1951''', the tumulus was designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's dimensions are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' * Diameter: ~50 meters * Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' * Width: ~55 meters * Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == Burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber dimensions: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == Artifacts == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifacts from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs * Sickle ×5 * Axe ×5 * Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs * Sickle ×3 * Axe ×2 * Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' * '''Horned helmet''' ×1 * '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were performed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a formal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Cultural Heritage == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館}} – Displays artifacts from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} * [[Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto)|Horned helmet]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuruyama Kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] Height: 7.5 m (rear circular part) | burial = {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (Vertical pit stone chamber, wooden coffin inside) | artifacts = Human remains, armor, helmets, numerous grave goods | construction_period = Mid-5th century | designated_as = {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|ja|群馬県指定史跡}} (1951) }} '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' (つるやまこふん) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, or keyhole-shaped tumulus, and has been designated as a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == Tsuruyama Kofun is a '''large keyhole-shaped tumulus''' built on the southeastern edge of the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地}}, west of the {{ill|Kanayama Hills|ja|金山丘陵}} in eastern Gunma Prefecture. The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. Additionally, a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}) was found to the north of the stone chamber. Excavations revealed '''human remains''', '''two helmets''', '''three cuirasses''', and numerous '''iron and stone objects'''. It is considered significant in understanding the '''political landscape of eastern Gunma''', particularly in relation to the nearby '''Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun''', the largest kofun in eastern Japan. In '''1951''', the tumulus was designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's dimensions are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' * Diameter: ~50 meters * Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' * Width: ~55 meters * Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == Burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber dimensions: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == Artifacts == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifacts from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs * Sickle ×5 * Axe ×5 * Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs * Sickle ×3 * Axe ×2 * Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' * '''Horned helmet''' ×1 * '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Cultural Heritage == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of {{Infobox Kofun | name = {{ill|Tsuruyama Kofun|ja|鶴山古墳}} | image = [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus mound (front part on the right, rear part on the left) | alternative_name = Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun | location = {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|19|27.08|N|139|20|25.88|E|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Keyhole-shaped tomb) | size = Mound length: 95 m
Height: 7.5 m (rear circular part) | burial = {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (Vertical pit stone chamber, wooden coffin inside) | artifacts = Human remains, armor, helmets, numerous grave goods | construction_period = Mid-5th century | designated_as = {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|ja|群馬県指定史跡}} (1951) }} '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' (つるやまこふん) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, or keyhole-shaped tumulus, and has been designated as a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == Tsuruyama Kofun is a '''large keyhole-shaped tumulus''' built on the southeastern edge of the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地}}, west of the {{ill|Kanayama Hills|ja|金山丘陵}} in eastern Gunma Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage, especially to its front section, and an excavation survey was conducted in '''1948'''. The mound does '''not''' feature a stone covering ({{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) or '''hollow clay figures''' ({{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}). A moat encircles the mound. The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. Additionally, a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}) was found to the north of the stone chamber. Excavations revealed '''human remains''', '''two helmets''', '''three cuirasses''', and numerous '''iron and stone objects'''. The tumulus is estimated to date to the '''mid-5th century''', within the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is considered significant in understanding the '''political landscape of eastern Gunma''', particularly in relation to the nearby '''Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun''', the largest kofun in eastern Japan. In '''1951''', the tumulus was designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's dimensions are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' * Diameter: ~50 meters * Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' * Width: ~55 meters * Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == Burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber dimensions: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == Artifacts == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifacts from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs * Sickle ×5 * Axe ×5 * Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs * Sickle ×3 * Axe ×2 * Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' * '''Horned helmet''' ×1 * '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were performed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a formal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Cultural Heritage == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館}} – Displays artifacts from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} * [[Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto)|Horned helmet]] * [[List of Historic Sites The chamber dimensions: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == Artifacts == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifacts from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs * Sickle ×5 * Axe ×5 * Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs * Sickle ×3 * Axe ×2 * Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' * '''Horned helmet''' ×1 * '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were performed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a formal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Cultural Heritage == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館}} – Displays artifacts from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} * [[Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto)|Horned helmet]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuruyama Kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Gunma Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]

{{Infobox Kofun | name = {{ill|Tsuruyama Kofun|ja|鶴山古墳}} | image = [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus mound (front part on the right, rear part on the left) | alternative_name = Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun | location = {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|19|27.08|N|139|20|25.88|E|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (Keyhole-shaped tomb) | size = Mound length: 95 m
Height: 7.5 m (rear circular part) | burial = {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (Vertical pit stone chamber, wooden coffin inside) | artifacts = Human remains, armor, helmets, numerous grave goods | construction_period = Mid-5th century | designated_as = {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|ja|群馬県指定史跡}} (1951) }} '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' (つるやまこふん) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, or keyhole-shaped tumulus, and has been designated as a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == Tsuruyama Kofun is a '''large keyhole-shaped tumulus''' built on the southeastern edge of the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地}}, west of the {{ill|Kanayama Hills|ja|金山丘陵}} in eastern Gunma Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage, especially to its front section, and an excavation survey was conducted in '''1948'''. The mound does '''not''' feature a stone covering ({{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) or '''hollow clay figures''' ({{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}). A moat encircles the mound. The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. Additionally, a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}) was found to the north of the stone chamber. Excavations revealed '''human remains''', '''two helmets''', '''three cuirasses''', and numerous '''iron and stone objects'''. The tumulus is estimated to date to the '''mid-5th century''', within the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is considered significant in understanding the '''political landscape of eastern Gunma''', particularly in relation to the nearby '''Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun''', the largest kofun in eastern Japan. In '''1951''', the tumulus was designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's dimensions are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' * Diameter: ~50 meters * Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' * Width: ~55 meters * Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == Burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber dimensions: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == Artifacts == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifacts from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs * Sickle ×5 * Axe ×5 * Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs * Sickle ×3 * Axe ×2 * Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' * '''Horned helmet''' ×1 * '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were performed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a formal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022.
