Kofun mound (front part on the right, rear part on the left) | alternate_name = Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun | location = {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|19|27.08|N|139|20|25.88|E|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} (Keyhole-shaped tomb) | area = Mound length: 95 m
Height: 7.5 m (rear circular part) | burial = {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (Vertical pit stone chamber, wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Human remains, armor, helmets, numerous grave goods | built = Mid-5th century | designation1 = {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|ja|群馬県指定史跡|12=draft|12=draft}} (1951) }} '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' (つるやまこふん) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}, or keyhole-shaped tumulus, and has been Historic Site1 as a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == Tsuruyama Kofun is a '''large keyhole-shaped tumulus''' built on the southeastern edge of the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地|12=draft|12=draft}}, west of the {{ill|Kanayama Hills|ja|金山丘陵|12=draft|12=draft}} in eastern Gunma Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage, especially to its front section, and an excavation survey was conducted in '''1948'''. The mound does '''not''' feature a stone covering ({{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}}) or '''hollow clay figures''' ({{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}). A moat encircles the mound. The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. Additionally, a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}) was found to the north of the stone chamber. Excavations revealed '''human remains''', '''two helmets''', '''three cuirasses''', and numerous '''iron and stone objects'''. The tumulus is estimated to date to the '''mid-5th century''', within the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is considered significant in understanding the '''political landscape of eastern Gunma''', particularly in relation to the nearby '''Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun''', the largest kofun in eastern Japan. In '''1951''', the tumulus was Historic Site1 a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄|12=draft|12=draft}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's area are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' ** Diameter: ~50 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' ** Width: ~55 meters ** Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber area: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == burial goods == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] burial goods excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×5 ** Axe ×5 ** Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×3 ** Axe ×2 ** Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' ** '''Horned helmet''' ×1 ** '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft|12=draft}} 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} – Displays burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuruyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Ota City Website – Tsuruyama Kofun] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsuruyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tsuru Kofun]] [[Category:鶴山古墳]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuru Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]] [[ja:鶴山古墳]] [[sn:suru Kofun]]

This aligns with historical records in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were pertypeed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a typeal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} – Displays burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuruyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Ota City Website – Tsuruyama Kofun] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsuruyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tsuru Kofun]] [[Category:鶴山古墳]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuru Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]] [[ja:鶴山古墳]] [[sn:suru Kofun]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|竪穴式石室|" to "|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun | name = {{ill|Tsuruyama Kofun|ja|鶴山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} | image = [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun mound (front part on the right, rear part on the left) | alternate_name = Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun | location = {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|19|27.08|N|139|20|25.88|E|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} (Keyhole-shaped tomb) | area = Mound length: 95 m
Height: 7.5 m (rear circular part) | burial = {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (Vertical pit stone chamber, wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Human remains, armor, helmets, numerous grave goods | built = Mid-5th century | designation1 = {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|ja|群馬県指定史跡|12=draft|12=draft}} (1951) }} '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' (つるやまこふん) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}, or keyhole-shaped tumulus, and has been Historic Site1 as a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == Tsuruyama Kofun is a '''large keyhole-shaped tumulus''' built on the southeastern edge of the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地|12=draft|12=draft}}, west of the {{ill|Kanayama Hills|ja|金山丘陵|12=draft|12=draft}} in eastern Gunma Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage, especially to its front section, and an excavation survey was conducted in '''1948'''. The mound does '''not''' feature a stone covering ({{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}}) or '''hollow clay figures''' ({{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}). A moat encircles the mound. The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. Additionally, a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}) was found to the north of the stone chamber. Excavations revealed '''human remains''', '''two helmets''', '''three cuirasses''', and numerous '''iron and stone objects'''. The tumulus is estimated to date to the '''mid-5th century''', within the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is considered significant in understanding the '''political landscape of eastern Gunma''', particularly in relation to the nearby '''Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun''', the largest kofun in eastern Japan. In '''1951''', the tumulus was Historic Site1 a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄|12=draft|12=draft}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's area are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' ** Diameter: ~50 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' ** Width: ~55 meters ** Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber area: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == burial goods == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] burial goods excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×5 ** Axe ×5 ** Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×3 ** Axe ×2 ** Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' ** '''Horned helmet''' ×1 ** '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft|12=draft}} 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were pertypeed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a typeal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} – Displays burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuruyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Ota City Website – Tsuruyama Kofun] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsuruyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tsuru Kofun]] [[Category:鶴山古墳]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuru Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]] [[ja:鶴山古墳]] [[sn:suru Kofun]]

revision.comment: 10 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:鶴山古墳 up to rev 100579508
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 鶴山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = 島之郷村3号墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[太田市]]鳥山上町2140ほか |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 19|緯度秒 = 27.08 |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 20|経度秒 = 25.88 |ISO = JP-10 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長95m
高さ7.5m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = [[竪穴式石室]]（内部に木棺） |出土品 = 人骨・短甲・冑ほか副葬品多数 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 群馬県指定史跡「鶴山古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Gunma |アイコン = 前方後円墳-南東 |ラベル = 鶴山古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''鶴山古墳'''（つるやまこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[太田市]]鳥山上町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。群馬県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 群馬県東部、金山丘陵西側の大間々扇状地の東南端部に築造された大型前方後円墳である{{Sfn|鶴山古墳（平凡社）|1987}}。現在までに前方部は大きく削平されているほか、[[1948年]]（[[昭和]]23年）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を南東方向に向ける。墳丘外表で[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]は認められていない{{Sfn|鶴山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘周囲には周濠が巡らされる{{Sfn|鶴山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。埋葬施設は後円部中央における[[竪穴式石室]]で、内部に木棺が据えられるほか、石室外の北側には[[粘土槨]]が認められる。石室内からは人骨や冑2・短甲3のほか多数の鉄製品・石製模造品が、粘土槨からは鉄鉾・鉄鏃が検出されている。ただし頭骨・装身具類・埴輪が発見されない点では特異な様相になる。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]中葉頃と推定される{{Sfn|鶴山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。東日本最大の古墳である[[太田天神山古墳]]（太田市内ケ島町）の存在を考え合わせて、群馬県東部の政治情勢を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|鶴山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 古墳域は、[[1951年]]（昭和26年）に群馬県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1938年]]（[[昭和]]13年）の『上毛古墳綜覧』に「島之郷村第3号墳」として登載。 * [[1948年]]（昭和23年）、埋葬施設の発掘調査（[[尾崎喜左雄]]ら）{{Sfn|鶴山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * [[1951年]]（昭和26年）10月5日、群馬県指定史跡に指定。 == 墳丘 == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|墳丘横景}}{{small|左に後円部、右に前方部。}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|鶴山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 墳丘長：現状102メートル{{Sfn|鶴山古墳（平凡社）|1987}}{{Sfn|鶴山古墳（古墳）|1989}}、推定復元約95メートル（耕作等による改変を加味） * 後円部 ** 直径：約50メートル ** 高さ：7.5メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：約55メートル ** 高さ：3メートル Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては、後円部中央において[[竪穴式石室]]が構築されており、石室主軸を東北東方向とする。石室は長さ2.8メートル・東端幅0.77メートル・西端幅0.58メートル・深さ0.6メートルを測る{{Sfn|鶴山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 石室の石材は扁平割石で、縦置きによって構築される{{Sfn|鶴山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。両側壁は5石、両端部は1石である{{Sfn|鶴山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。石室内には木棺が据えられ、木棺内外では人骨（上肢骨・下肢骨）のほか、甲冑を始めとする多数の副葬品が検出されている。 石室西端の北側には[[粘土槨]]が構築されており、鉄鉾・鉄鏃が出土している{{Sfn|鶴山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 出土品 == [[ファイル:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|出土品}}{{small|[[群馬県立歴史博物館]]展示（他画像も同様）。}}]] 1948年（昭和23年）の調査で検出された副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|鶴山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 ; 木棺内 * 大刀 1点 - 上体側部。 * 鉄製農工具類 - 下肢部。 ** 鎌 5 ** 斧 5 ** 鉇 6以上 * 石製模造品 - 下肢部。 ** 鎌 3 ** 斧 2 ** 刀子 21 ; 木棺・石室側部間 * 剣 2 * 大刀 3 ; 石室東端部（頭部） * 短甲 3 - 横矧板鋲留短甲2、長方形板革綴短甲1。横矧板鋲留短甲は冑・頸甲・肩甲とセット関係で、長方形板革綴短甲は横矧板鋲留短甲に先行する。 * 衝角付冑 1 * 眉庇付冑 1 * 頸甲 2 * 肩甲 2 ; 木棺上面 * 盾 - 月日貝7枚を飾る杉綾文地黒漆塗赤色塗彩隅金具付。 ; 石室外（粘土槨内） * 鉄鉾 5 * 鉄鏃 約100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|1号短甲 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|衝角付冑 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|眉庇付冑 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|鉄製品 == 考証 == 鶴山古墳で頭骨・装身具類は発見されていないことについて、『日本書紀』では[[上毛野氏]]の一族が[[朝鮮半島]]に出征したことが記されているため、被葬者は外地で戦死して首の無い状態で帰国し、その異常性ゆえに葬儀儀礼も行われなかったとする説がある若狭徹「前方後円墳の社会的機能に関する一考察」吉村武彦 編『律令制国家の理念と実像』八木書店、2022年 ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8 P107-109.。 == 文化財 == === 群馬県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 鶴山古墳 - 1951年（昭和26年）10月5日指定[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html 鶴山古墳]（太田市ホームページ）。史跡説明板。。 == 関連施設 == * [[群馬県立歴史博物館]]（高崎市綿貫町） - 鶴山古墳の出土品（群馬大学所有）を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（群馬県教育委員会・太田市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=鶴山古墳|title=群馬県の地名|volume=[[日本歴史地名大系]]10|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|鶴山古墳（平凡社）|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[大塚初重]]|year=1989|chapter=鶴山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|鶴山古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=梅澤重昭|year=2002|chapter=鶴山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|鶴山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=右島和夫|year=1986|chapter=鶴山古墳出土遺物の基礎調査I|title=群馬県立歴史博物館調査報告書2|publisher=群馬県立歴史博物館|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=右島和夫|year=1987|chapter=鶴山古墳出土遺物の基礎調査II|title=群馬県立歴史博物館調査報告書3|publisher=群馬県立歴史博物館|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=右島和夫|year=1988|chapter=鶴山古墳出土遺物の基礎調査III|title=群馬県立歴史博物館調査報告書4|publisher=群馬県立歴史博物館|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=右島和夫|year=1989|chapter=鶴山古墳出土遺物の基礎調査IV|title=群馬県立歴史博物館調査報告書5|publisher=群馬県立歴史博物館|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=右島和夫|year=1990|chapter=鶴山古墳出土遺物の基礎調査V|title=群馬県立歴史博物館調査報告書6|publisher=群馬県立歴史博物館|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=右島和夫|year=1996|chapter=鶴山古墳出土遺物の基礎調査VI|title=群馬県立歴史博物館調査報告書7|publisher=群馬県立歴史博物館|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=1996|chapter=鶴山古墳|title=太田市史|volume= 通史編 原始古代|publisher=太田市|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[太田天神山古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tsuruyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html 鶴山古墳] - 太田市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:つるやまこふん}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:太田市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:群馬県指定史跡]]

上体側部。 * 鉄製農工具類 - 下肢部。 ** 鎌 5 ** 斧 5 ** 鉇 6以上 * 石製模造品 - 下肢部。 ** 鎌 3 ** 斧 2 ** 刀子 21 ; 木棺・石室側部間 * 剣 2 * 大刀 3 ; 石室東端部（頭部） * 短甲 3 - 横矧板鋲留短甲2、長方形板革綴短甲1。横矧板鋲留短甲は冑・頸甲・肩甲とセット関係で、長方形板革綴短甲は横矧板鋲留短甲に先行する。 * 衝角付冑 1 * 眉庇付冑 1 * 頸甲 2 * 肩甲 2 ; 木棺上面 * 盾 - 月日貝7枚を飾る杉綾文地黒漆塗赤色塗彩隅金具付。 ; 石室外（粘土槨内） * 鉄鉾 5 * 鉄鏃 約100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|1号短甲 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|衝角付冑 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|眉庇付冑 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|鉄製品 == 考証 == 鶴山古墳で頭骨・装身具類は発見されていないことについて、『日本書紀』では[[上毛野氏]]の一族が[[朝鮮半島]]に出征したことが記されているため、被葬者は外地で戦死して首の無い状態で帰国し、その異常性ゆえに葬儀儀礼も行われなかったとする説がある若狭徹「前方後円墳の社会的機能に関する一考察」吉村武彦 編『律令制国家の理念と実像』八木書店、2022年 ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8 P107-109.。 == 文化財 == === 群馬県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 鶴山古墳 - 1951年（昭和26年）10月5日指定[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html 鶴山古墳]（太田市ホームページ）。史跡説明板。。 == 関連施設 == * [[群馬県立歴史博物館]]（高崎市綿貫町） - 鶴山古墳の出土品（群馬大学所有）を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（群馬県教育委員会・太田市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=鶴山古墳|title=群馬県の地名|volume=[[日本歴史地名大系]]10|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|鶴山古墳（平凡社）|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[大塚初重]]|year=1989|chapter=鶴山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|鶴山古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=梅澤重昭|year=2002|chapter=鶴山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|鶴山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=右島和夫|year=1986|chapter=鶴山古墳出土遺物の基礎調査I|title=群馬県立歴史博物館調査報告書2|publisher=群馬県立歴史博物館|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=右島和夫|year=1987|chapter=鶴山古墳出土遺物の基礎調査II|title=群馬県立歴史博物館調査報告書3|publisher=群馬県立歴史博物館|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=右島和夫|year=1988|chapter=鶴山古墳出土遺物の基礎調査III|title=群馬県立歴史博物館調査報告書4|publisher=群馬県立歴史博物館|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=右島和夫|year=1989|chapter=鶴山古墳出土遺物の基礎調査IV|title=群馬県立歴史博物館調査報告書5|publisher=群馬県立歴史博物館|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=右島和夫|year=1990|chapter=鶴山古墳出土遺物の基礎調査V|title=群馬県立歴史博物館調査報告書6|publisher=群馬県立歴史博物館|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=右島和夫|year=1996|chapter=鶴山古墳出土遺物の基礎調査VI|title=群馬県立歴史博物館調査報告書7|publisher=群馬県立歴史博物館|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=1996|chapter=鶴山古墳|title=太田市史|volume= 通史編 原始古代|publisher=太田市|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[太田天神山古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tsuruyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html 鶴山古墳] - This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun | name = {{ill|Tsuruyama Kofun|ja|鶴山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | image = [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun mound (front part on the right, rear part on the left) | alternate_name = Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun | location = {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|19|27.08|N|139|20|25.88|E|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Keyhole-shaped tomb) | area = Mound length: 95 m
Height: 7.5 m (rear circular part) | burial = {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Vertical pit stone chamber, wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Human remains, armor, helmets, numerous grave goods | built = Mid-5th century | designation1 = {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|ja|群馬県指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1951) }} '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' (つるやまこふん) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or keyhole-shaped tumulus, and has been Historic Site1 as a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == Tsuruyama Kofun is a '''large keyhole-shaped tumulus''' built on the southeastern edge of the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, west of the {{ill|Kanayama Hills|ja|金山丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in eastern Gunma Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage, especially to its front section, and an excavation survey was conducted in '''1948'''. The mound does '''not''' feature a stone covering ({{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) or '''hollow clay figures''' ({{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). A moat encircles the mound. The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. Additionally, a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was found to the north of the stone chamber. Excavations revealed '''human remains''', '''two helmets''', '''three cuirasses''', and numerous '''iron and stone objects'''. The tumulus is estimated to date to the '''mid-5th century''', within the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is considered significant in understanding the '''political landscape of eastern Gunma''', particularly in relation to the nearby '''Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun''', the largest kofun in eastern Japan. In '''1951''', the tumulus was Historic Site1 a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's area are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' ** Diameter: ~50 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' ** Width: ~55 meters ** Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber area: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == burial goods == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}}]] burial goods excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×5 ** Axe ×5 ** Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×3 ** Axe ×2 ** Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' ** '''Horned helmet''' ×1 ** '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were pertypeed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a typeal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Displays burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuruyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Ota City Website – Tsuruyama Kofun] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsuruyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tsuru Kofun]] [[Category:鶴山古墳]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuru Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]] [[ja:鶴山古墳]] [[sn:suru Kofun]] {{translated page|ja|鶴山古墳|version=100579508|comment=Imported full ja history}}

In '''1951''', the tumulus was Historic Site1 a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's area are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' ** Diameter: ~50 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' ** Width: ~55 meters ** Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber area: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == burial goods == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}}]] burial goods excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×5 ** Axe ×5 ** Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×3 ** Axe ×2 ** Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' ** '''Horned helmet''' ×1 ** '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were pertypeed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a typeal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Displays burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuruyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Ota City Website – Tsuruyama Kofun] {{citation needed|Tsuruyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴山古墳|version=100579508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tsuru Kofun]] [[Category:鶴山古墳]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuru Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]] [[ja:鶴山古墳]] [[sn:suru Kofun]]

Kofun mound (front part on the right, rear part on the left) | alternate_name = Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun | location = {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|19|27.08|N|139|20|25.88|E|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Keyhole-shaped tomb) | area = Mound length: 95 m
Height: 7.5 m (rear circular part) | burial = {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Vertical pit stone chamber, wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Human remains, armor, helmets, numerous grave goods | built = Mid-5th century | designation1 = {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|ja|群馬県指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1951) }} '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' (つるやまこふん) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or keyhole-shaped tumulus, and has been Historic Site1 as a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == Tsuruyama Kofun is a '''large keyhole-shaped tumulus''' built on the southeastern edge of the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, west of the {{ill|Kanayama Hills|ja|金山丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in eastern Gunma Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage, especially to its front section, and an excavation survey was conducted in '''1948'''. The mound does '''not''' feature a stone covering ({{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) or '''hollow clay figures''' ({{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). A moat encircles the mound. The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. Additionally, a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was found to the north of the stone chamber. Excavations revealed '''human remains''', '''two helmets''', '''three cuirasses''', and numerous '''iron and stone objects'''. The tumulus is estimated to date to the '''mid-5th century''', within the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is considered significant in understanding the '''political landscape of eastern Gunma''', particularly in relation to the nearby '''Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun''', the largest kofun in eastern Japan. In '''1951''', the tumulus was Historic Site1 a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's area are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' ** Diameter: ~50 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' ** Width: ~55 meters ** Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber area: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == burial goods == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}}]] burial goods excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×5 ** Axe ×5 ** Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×3 ** Axe ×2 ** Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' ** '''Horned helmet''' ×1 ** '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were pertypeed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a typeal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Displays burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuruyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Ota City Website – Tsuruyama Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴山古墳|version=100579508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tsuru Kofun]] [[Category:鶴山古墳]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuru Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]] [[ja:鶴山古墳]] [[sn:suru Kofun]]

This aligns with historical records in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were pertypeed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a typeal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Displays burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuruyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Ota City Website – Tsuruyama Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴山古墳|version=100579508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tsuru Kofun]] [[Category:鶴山古墳]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuru Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]] [[ja:鶴山古墳]] [[sn:suru Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun | name = {{ill|Tsuruyama Kofun|ja|鶴山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | image = [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun mound (front part on the right, rear part on the left) | alternate_name = Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun | location = {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|19|27.08|N|139|20|25.88|E|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Keyhole-shaped tomb) | area = Mound length: 95 m
Height: 7.5 m (rear circular part) | burial = {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Vertical pit stone chamber, wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Human remains, armor, helmets, numerous grave goods | built = Mid-5th century | designation1 = {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|ja|群馬県指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1951) }} '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' (つるやまこふん) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or keyhole-shaped tumulus, and has been Historic Site1 as a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == Tsuruyama Kofun is a '''large keyhole-shaped tumulus''' built on the southeastern edge of the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, west of the {{ill|Kanayama Hills|ja|金山丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in eastern Gunma Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage, especially to its front section, and an excavation survey was conducted in '''1948'''. The mound does '''not''' feature a stone covering ({{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) or '''hollow clay figures''' ({{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). A moat encircles the mound. The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. Additionally, a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was found to the north of the stone chamber. Excavations revealed '''human remains''', '''two helmets''', '''three cuirasses''', and numerous '''iron and stone objects'''. The tumulus is estimated to date to the '''mid-5th century''', within the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is considered significant in understanding the '''political landscape of eastern Gunma''', particularly in relation to the nearby '''Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun''', the largest kofun in eastern Japan. In '''1951''', the tumulus was Historic Site1 a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's area are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' ** Diameter: ~50 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' ** Width: ~55 meters ** Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber area: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == burial goods == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}}]] burial goods excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×5 ** Axe ×5 ** Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×3 ** Axe ×2 ** Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' ** '''Horned helmet''' ×1 ** '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were pertypeed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a typeal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Displays burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuruyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Ota City Website – Tsuruyama Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴山古墳|version=100579508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tsuru Kofun]] [[Category:鶴山古墳]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]] [[ja:鶴山古墳]] [[sn:suru Kofun]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun | name = {{ill|Tsuruyama Kofun|ja|鶴山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | image = [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun mound (front part on the right, rear part on the left) | alternate_name = Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun | location = {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|19|27.08|N|139|20|25.88|E|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Keyhole-shaped tomb) | area = Mound length: 95 m
Height: 7.5 m (rear circular part) | burial = {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Vertical pit stone chamber, wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Human remains, armor, helmets, numerous grave goods | built = Mid-5th century | designation1 = {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|ja|群馬県指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1951) }} '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' (つるやまこふん) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or keyhole-shaped tumulus, and has been Historic Site1 as a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == Tsuruyama Kofun is a '''large keyhole-shaped tumulus''' built on the southeastern edge of the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, west of the {{ill|Kanayama Hills|ja|金山丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in eastern Gunma Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage, especially to its front section, and an excavation survey was conducted in '''1948'''. The mound does '''not''' feature a stone covering ({{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) or '''hollow clay figures''' ({{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). A moat encircles the mound. The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. Additionally, a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was found to the north of the stone chamber. Excavations revealed '''human remains''', '''two helmets''', '''three cuirasses''', and numerous '''iron and stone objects'''. The tumulus is estimated to date to the '''mid-5th century''', within the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is considered significant in understanding the '''political landscape of eastern Gunma''', particularly in relation to the nearby '''Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun''', the largest kofun in eastern Japan. In '''1951''', the tumulus was Historic Site1 a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's area are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' ** Diameter: ~50 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' ** Width: ~55 meters ** Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber area: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == burial goods == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}}]] burial goods excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×5 ** Axe ×5 ** Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×3 ** Axe ×2 ** Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' ** '''Horned helmet''' ×1 ** '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were pertypeed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a typeal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Displays burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuruyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Ota City Website – Tsuruyama Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴山古墳|version=100579508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tsuru Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]] [[ja:鶴山古墳]] [[sn:suru Kofun]]

Height: 7.5 m (rear circular part) | burial = {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Vertical pit stone chamber, wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Human remains, armor, helmets, numerous grave goods | built = Mid-5th century | designation1 = {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|ja|群馬県指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|qq=}} (1951) }} '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' (つるやまこふん) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} Kofun located in {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota, Gunma|qq=}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, or keyhole-shaped tumulus, and has been Historic Site1 as a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == Tsuruyama Kofun is a '''large keyhole-shaped tumulus''' built on the southeastern edge of the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmama alluvial fan|qq=}}, west of the {{ill|Kanayama Hills|ja|金山丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Hills|qq=}} in eastern Gunma Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage, especially to its front section, and an excavation survey was conducted in '''1948'''. The mound does '''not''' feature a stone covering ({{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}}) or '''hollow clay figures''' ({{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}}). A moat encircles the mound. The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. Additionally, a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}) was found to the north of the stone chamber. Excavations revealed '''human remains''', '''two helmets''', '''three cuirasses''', and numerous '''iron and stone objects'''. The tumulus is estimated to date to the '''mid-5th century''', within the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. It is considered significant in understanding the '''political landscape of eastern Gunma''', particularly in relation to the nearby '''Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun''', the largest kofun in eastern Japan. In '''1951''', the tumulus was Historic Site1 a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ozaki Kizuo|qq=}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's area are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' ** Diameter: ~50 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' ** Width: ~55 meters ** Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber area: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nendōkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == burial goods == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|qq=}}.}}]] burial goods excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×5 ** Axe ×5 ** Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×3 ** Axe ×2 ** Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' ** '''Horned helmet''' ×1 ** '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were pertypeed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a typeal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|qq=}} – Displays burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuruyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Ota City Website – Tsuruyama Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴山古墳|version=100579508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tsuru Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]] [[ja:鶴山古墳]] [[sn:suru Kofun]]
