Kofun mound (front part on the right, rear part on the left) | alternate_name = Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun | location = {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota, Gunma|qq=}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|19|27.08|N|139|20|25.88|E|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} (Keyhole-shaped tomb) | area = Mound length: 95 m
Height: 7.5 m (rear circular part) | burial = {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Vertical pit stone chamber, wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Human remains, armor, helmets, numerous grave goods | built = Mid-5th century | designation1 = {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|ja|群馬県指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|qq=}} (1951) }} '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' (つるやまこふん) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} Kofun located in {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota, Gunma|qq=}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, or keyhole-shaped tumulus, and has been Historic Site1 as a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == Tsuruyama Kofun is a '''large keyhole-shaped tumulus''' built on the southeastern edge of the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmama alluvial fan|qq=}}, west of the {{ill|Kanayama Hills|ja|金山丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Hills|qq=}} in eastern Gunma Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage, especially to its front section, and an excavation survey was conducted in '''1948'''. The mound does '''not''' feature a stone covering ({{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}}) or '''hollow clay figures''' ({{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}}). A moat encircles the mound. The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. Additionally, a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}) was found to the north of the stone chamber. Excavations revealed '''human remains''', '''two helmets''', '''three cuirasses''', and numerous '''iron and stone objects'''. The tumulus is estimated to date to the '''mid-5th century''', within the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. It is considered significant in understanding the '''political landscape of eastern Gunma''', particularly in relation to the nearby '''Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun''', the largest kofun in eastern Japan. In '''1951''', the tumulus was Historic Site1 a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ozaki Kizuo|qq=}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's area are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' ** Diameter: ~50 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' ** Width: ~55 meters ** Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber area: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nendōkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == burial goods == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|qq=}}.}}]] burial goods excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×5 ** Axe ×5 ** Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×3 ** Axe ×2 ** Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' ** '''Horned helmet''' ×1 ** '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were pertypeed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a typeal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|qq=}} – Displays burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuruyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Ota City Website – Tsuruyama Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴山古墳|version=100579508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tsuru Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]] [[ja:鶴山古墳]] [[sn:suru Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. This aligns with historical records in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were pertypeed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a typeal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|qq=}} – Displays burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuruyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Ota City Website – Tsuruyama Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴山古墳|version=100579508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tsuru Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]] [[ja:鶴山古墳]] [[sn:suru Kofun]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove sole member from [[Category:Tsuru Kofun]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun | name = {{ill|Tsuruyama Kofun|ja|鶴山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruyama Kofun|qq=}} | image = [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun mound (front part on the right, rear part on the left) | alternate_name = Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun | location = {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota, Gunma|qq=}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|19|27.08|N|139|20|25.88|E|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} (Keyhole-shaped tomb) | area = Mound length: 95 m
Height: 7.5 m (rear circular part) | burial = {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Vertical pit stone chamber, wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Human remains, armor, helmets, numerous grave goods | built = Mid-5th century | designation1 = {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|ja|群馬県指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|qq=}} (1951) }} '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' (つるやまこふん) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} Kofun located in {{ill|Ota, Gunma|ja|太田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota, Gunma|qq=}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, or keyhole-shaped tumulus, and has been Historic Site1 as a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == Tsuruyama Kofun is a '''large keyhole-shaped tumulus''' built on the southeastern edge of the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmama alluvial fan|qq=}}, west of the {{ill|Kanayama Hills|ja|金山丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Hills|qq=}} in eastern Gunma Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage, especially to its front section, and an excavation survey was conducted in '''1948'''. The mound does '''not''' feature a stone covering ({{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}}) or '''hollow clay figures''' ({{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}}). A moat encircles the mound. The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. Additionally, a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}) was found to the north of the stone chamber. Excavations revealed '''human remains''', '''two helmets''', '''three cuirasses''', and numerous '''iron and stone objects'''. The tumulus is estimated to date to the '''mid-5th century''', within the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. It is considered significant in understanding the '''political landscape of eastern Gunma''', particularly in relation to the nearby '''Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun''', the largest kofun in eastern Japan. In '''1951''', the tumulus was Historic Site1 a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ozaki Kizuo|qq=}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's area are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' ** Diameter: ~50 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' ** Width: ~55 meters ** Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber area: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nendōkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == burial goods == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|qq=}}.}}]] burial goods excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×5 ** Axe ×5 ** Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×3 ** Axe ×2 ** Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' ** '''Horned helmet''' ×1 ** '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were pertypeed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a typeal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|ja|群馬県立歴史博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|qq=}} – Displays burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuruyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Ota City Website – Tsuruyama Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴山古墳|version=100579508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]] [[ja:鶴山古墳]] [[sn:suru Kofun]]

Kofun mound (front part on the right, rear part on the left) | alternate_name = Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun | location = [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ota, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|19|27.08|N|139|20|25.88|E|display=inline}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (Keyhole-shaped tomb) | area = Mound length: 95 m
Height: 7.5 m (rear circular part) | burial = {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Vertical pit stone chamber, wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Human remains, armor, helmets, numerous grave goods | built = Mid-5th century | designation1 = {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|ja|群馬県指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|qq=}} (1951) }} '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' (つるやまこふん) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ota, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is classified as a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], or keyhole-shaped tumulus, and has been Historic Site1 as a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == Tsuruyama Kofun is a '''large keyhole-shaped tumulus''' built on the southeastern edge of the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmama alluvial fan|qq=}}, west of the {{ill|Kanayama Hills|ja|金山丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Hills|qq=}} in eastern Gunma Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage, especially to its front section, and an excavation survey was conducted in '''1948'''. The mound does '''not''' feature a stone covering ([[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]) or '''hollow clay figures''' ([[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]). A moat encircles the mound. The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. Additionally, a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}) was found to the north of the stone chamber. Excavations revealed '''human remains''', '''two helmets''', '''three cuirasses''', and numerous '''iron and stone objects'''. The tumulus is estimated to date to the '''mid-5th century''', within the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It is considered significant in understanding the '''political landscape of eastern Gunma''', particularly in relation to the nearby '''Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun''', the largest kofun in eastern Japan. In '''1951''', the tumulus was Historic Site1 a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ozaki Kizuo|qq=}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's area are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' ** Diameter: ~50 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' ** Width: ~55 meters ** Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber area: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nendōkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == burial goods == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at [[:en:Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History]].}}]] burial goods excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×5 ** Axe ×5 ** Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×3 ** Axe ×2 ** Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' ** '''Horned helmet''' ×1 ** '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were pertypeed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a typeal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * [[:en:Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History]] – Displays burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * [[:en:Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuruyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Ota City Website – Tsuruyama Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴山古墳|version=100579508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]] [[ja:鶴山古墳]] [[sn:suru Kofun]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Infobox Kofun" to "Infobox ancient site"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = {{ill|Tsuruyama Kofun|ja|鶴山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruyama Kofun|qq=}} | image = [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun mound (front part on the right, rear part on the left) | alternate_name = Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun | location = [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ota, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|19|27.08|N|139|20|25.88|E|display=inline}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (Keyhole-shaped tomb) | area = Mound length: 95 m
Height: 7.5 m (rear circular part) | burial = {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Vertical pit stone chamber, wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Human remains, armor, helmets, numerous grave goods | built = Mid-5th century | designation1 = {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|ja|群馬県指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|qq=}} (1951) }} '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' (つるやまこふん) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ota, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is classified as a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], or keyhole-shaped tumulus, and has been Historic Site1 as a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == Tsuruyama Kofun is a '''large keyhole-shaped tumulus''' built on the southeastern edge of the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmama alluvial fan|qq=}}, west of the {{ill|Kanayama Hills|ja|金山丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Hills|qq=}} in eastern Gunma Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage, especially to its front section, and an excavation survey was conducted in '''1948'''. The mound does '''not''' feature a stone covering ([[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]) or '''hollow clay figures''' ([[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]). A moat encircles the mound. The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. Additionally, a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}) was found to the north of the stone chamber. Excavations revealed '''human remains''', '''two helmets''', '''three cuirasses''', and numerous '''iron and stone objects'''. The tumulus is estimated to date to the '''mid-5th century''', within the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It is considered significant in understanding the '''political landscape of eastern Gunma''', particularly in relation to the nearby '''Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun''', the largest kofun in eastern Japan. In '''1951''', the tumulus was Historic Site1 a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ozaki Kizuo|qq=}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's area are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' ** Diameter: ~50 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' ** Width: ~55 meters ** Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber area: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nendōkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == burial goods == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at [[:en:Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History]].}}]] burial goods excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×5 ** Axe ×5 ** Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×3 ** Axe ×2 ** Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' ** '''Horned helmet''' ×1 ** '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were pertypeed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a typeal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * [[:en:Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History]] – Displays burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * [[:en:Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuruyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Ota City Website – Tsuruyama Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴山古墳|version=100579508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]] [[ja:鶴山古墳]] [[sn:suru Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = {{ill|Tsuruyama Kofun|ja|鶴山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruyama Kofun|qq=}} | image = [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun mound (front part on the right, rear part on the left) | alternate_name = Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun | location = [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ota, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|19|27.08|N|139|20|25.88|E|display=inline}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (Keyhole-shaped tomb) | area = Mound length: 95 m
Height: 7.5 m (rear circular part) | burial = {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Vertical pit stone chamber, wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Human remains, armor, helmets, numerous grave goods | built = Mid-5th century | designation1 = {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|ja|群馬県指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|qq=}} (1951) }} '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' (鶴山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ota, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is classified as a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], or keyhole-shaped tumulus, and has been Historic Site1 as a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == Tsuruyama Kofun is a '''large keyhole-shaped tumulus''' built on the southeastern edge of the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmama alluvial fan|qq=}}, west of the {{ill|Kanayama Hills|ja|金山丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Hills|qq=}} in eastern Gunma Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage, especially to its front section, and an excavation survey was conducted in '''1948'''. The mound does '''not''' feature a stone covering ([[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]) or '''hollow clay figures''' ([[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]). A moat encircles the mound. The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. Additionally, a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}) was found to the north of the stone chamber. Excavations revealed '''human remains''', '''two helmets''', '''three cuirasses''', and numerous '''iron and stone objects'''. The tumulus is estimated to date to the '''mid-5th century''', within the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It is considered significant in understanding the '''political landscape of eastern Gunma''', particularly in relation to the nearby '''Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun''', the largest kofun in eastern Japan. In '''1951''', the tumulus was Historic Site1 a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ozaki Kizuo|qq=}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's area are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' ** Diameter: ~50 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' ** Width: ~55 meters ** Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber area: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nendōkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == burial goods == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at [[:en:Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History]].}}]] burial goods excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×5 ** Axe ×5 ** Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×3 ** Axe ×2 ** Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' ** '''Horned helmet''' ×1 ** '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were pertypeed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a typeal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * [[:en:Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History]] – Displays burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * [[:en:Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuruyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Ota City Website – Tsuruyama Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴山古墳|version=100579508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]] [[ja:鶴山古墳]] [[sn:suru Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Kofun mound (front part on the right, rear part on the left) | alternate_name = Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun | location = [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ota, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|19|27.08|N|139|20|25.88|E|display=inline}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (Keyhole-shaped tomb) | area = Mound length: 95 m
Height: 7.5 m (rear circular part) | burial = {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Vertical pit stone chamber, wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Human remains, armor, helmets, numerous grave goods | built = Mid-5th century | designation1 = {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|ja|群馬県指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|qq=}} (1951) }} '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' (鶴山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ota, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is classified as a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], or keyhole-shaped tumulus, and has been Historic Site1 as a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == Tsuruyama Kofun is a '''large keyhole-shaped tumulus''' built on the southeastern edge of the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmama alluvial fan|qq=}}, west of the {{ill|Kanayama Hills|ja|金山丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Hills|qq=}} in eastern Gunma Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage, especially to its front section, and an excavation survey was conducted in '''1948'''. The mound does '''not''' feature a stone covering ([[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]) or '''hollow clay figures''' ([[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]). A moat encircles the mound. The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. Additionally, a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}) was found to the north of the stone chamber. Excavations revealed '''human remains''', '''two helmets''', '''three cuirasses''', and numerous '''iron and stone objects'''. The tumulus is estimated to date to the '''mid-5th century''', within the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It is considered significant in understanding the '''political landscape of eastern Gunma''', particularly in relation to the nearby '''Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun''', the largest kofun in eastern Japan. In '''1951''', the tumulus was Historic Site1 a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ozaki Kizuo|qq=}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's area are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' ** Diameter: ~50 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' ** Width: ~55 meters ** Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber area: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nendōkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == burial goods == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at [[:en:Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History]].}}]] burial goods excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×5 ** Axe ×5 ** Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×3 ** Axe ×2 ** Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' ** '''Horned helmet''' ×1 ** '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were pertypeed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a typeal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * [[:en:Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History]] – Displays burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * [[:en:Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuruyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Ota City Website – Tsuruyama Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴山古墳|version=100579508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]] [[ja:鶴山古墳]] [[sn:suru Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:太田市の歴史]] [[Category:群馬県の古墳]]

The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. Additionally, a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}) was found to the north of the stone chamber. Excavations revealed '''human remains''', '''two helmets''', '''three cuirasses''', and numerous '''iron and stone objects'''. The tumulus is estimated to date to the '''mid-5th century''', within the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It is considered significant in understanding the '''political landscape of eastern Gunma''', particularly in relation to the nearby '''Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun''', the largest kofun in eastern Japan. In '''1951''', the tumulus was Historic Site1 a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ozaki Kizuo|qq=}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's area are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' ** Diameter: ~50 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' ** Width: ~55 meters ** Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber area: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nendōkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == burial goods == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at [[:en:Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History]].}}]] burial goods excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×5 ** Axe ×5 ** Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×3 ** Axe ×2 ** Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' ** '''Horned helmet''' ×1 ** '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were pertypeed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a typeal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * [[:en:Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History]] – Displays burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * [[:en:Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuruyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Ota City Website – Tsuruyama Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴山古墳|version=100579508|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsuru Kofun]] [[ja:鶴山古墳]] [[sn:suru Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:太田市の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]]
