[[co:commons:Category:Tsuruyama Kofun]][[ja:鶴山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Tsuruyama Kofun]][[ja:鶴山古墳]] [[ja:鶴山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = {{ill|Tsuruyama Kofun|ja|鶴山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruyama Kofun|1=Tsuru Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q115519739}} | image = File:Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg | alternate_name = Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun | location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}; {{ill|Ota|WD=Q386179|1=Ōta, Gunma}} | coordinates = {{coord|36|19|27.08|N|139|20|25.88|E|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} (Keyhole-shaped tomb) | area = Mound length: 95 m
Height: 7.5 m (rear circular part) | burial = {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Vertical pit stone chamber, wooden coffin inside) | burial goods = Human remains, armor, helmets, numerous grave goods | built = Mid-5th century | designation1 = {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|ja|群馬県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefectural Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群馬県指定文化財一覧|1=Gunma Prefecture-designated Important Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609593}} (1951) }} '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' (鶴山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|en|Ōta, Gunma|WD=Q386179|lt=Ota, Gunma|1=Ōta, Gunma}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}}, or keyhole-shaped tumulus, and has been Historic Site1 as a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == Tsuruyama Kofun is a '''large keyhole-shaped tumulus''' built on the southeastern edge of the {{ill|Ōmama alluvial fan|ja|大間々扇状地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmama alluvial fan|1=Ōmama Alluvial Fan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439861}}, west of the {{ill|Kanayama Hills|ja|金山丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Hills|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in eastern Gunma Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage, especially to its front section, and an excavation survey was conducted in '''1948'''. The mound does '''not''' feature a stone covering ({{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}}) or '''hollow clay figures''' ({{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}}). A moat encircles the mound. The burial facility consists of a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' ({{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}) with a wooden coffin placed inside. Additionally, a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}) was found to the north of the stone chamber. Excavations revealed '''human remains''', '''two helmets''', '''three cuirasses''', and numerous '''iron and stone objects'''. The tumulus is estimated to date to the '''mid-5th century''', within the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. It is considered significant in understanding the '''political landscape of eastern Gunma''', particularly in relation to the nearby '''Ōta Tenjin-yama Kofun''', the largest kofun in eastern Japan. In '''1951''', the tumulus was Historic Site1 a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Excavation History == * '''1938''' – Listed in the *Jōmō Kofun Sōran* as "Shimanosato Village No. 3 Kofun." * '''1948''' – Excavation of the burial chamber led by {{ill|Ozaki Kizuo|ja|尾崎喜左雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ozaki Kizuo|1=Kisao Ozaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21698487}}. * '''1951''' – Designated a '''Gunma Prefectural Historic Site''' on October 5. == Mound Structure == [[File:Tsuruyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Side view of the tumulus}}{{small|Rear circular part on the left, front part on the right.}}]] The mound's area are as follows: * '''Total mound length''': Currently 102 meters (estimated reconstruction: 95 meters) * '''Rear circular section''' ** Diameter: ~50 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters * '''Front rectangular section''' ** Width: ~55 meters ** Height: 3 meters Tsuruyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear Tsuruyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front == burial Chamber == The '''burial chamber''' is a '''vertical pit stone chamber''' built in the center of the rear circular mound, aligned in an '''east-northeast direction'''. The chamber area: * '''Length''': 2.8 meters * '''Width''': 0.77 meters (east end), 0.58 meters (west end) * '''Depth''': 0.6 meters The '''walls are made of flat stones''' placed vertically, with '''five stones on each side''' and '''one stone at each end'''. Inside the chamber, a '''wooden coffin''' was placed, containing '''human remains (limb bones)''' and a '''large number of grave goods'''. Adjacent to the chamber, on the '''north side''', a '''clay coffin chamber''' ({{ill|nendōkaku|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nendōkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}) was discovered, containing '''iron spears''' and '''iron arrowheads'''. == burial goods == [[File:鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|en|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|WD=Q20982941|lt=Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|1=Gunma Prefectural Museum of History}}.}}]] burial goods excavated in 1948: ; Inside the wooden coffin * '''Dagger''' – Placed beside the upper body * '''Iron farming tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×5 ** Axe ×5 ** Chisel ×6+ * '''Stone replicas of tools''' – Placed near the lower limbs ** Sickle ×3 ** Axe ×2 ** Knife ×21 ; Between the coffin and the chamber walls * '''Swords''' ×2 * '''Daggers''' ×3 ; Near the head inside the chamber * '''Cuirasses''' ×3 (two riveted plate cuirasses, one rectangular plate-laced cuirass) * '''Helmets''' ** '''Horned helmet''' ×1 ** '''Visored helmet''' ×1 * '''Neck guards''' ×2 * '''Shoulder guards''' ×2 ; On top of the coffin * '''Shield''' – Black lacquered, red-painted with gold fittings, decorated with '''seven pearl oyster shells'''. ; Inside the clay coffin chamber * '''Iron spears''' ×5 * '''Iron arrowheads''' ~100 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 1号短甲.JPG|type 1 Cuirass 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 衝角付冑.JPG|{{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 眉庇付冑.JPG|Visored Helmet 鶴山古墳 (太田市) 出土 鉄製品.JPG|Iron objects == Interpretation == The '''absence of a skull and jewelry''' has led some scholars to speculate that the individual buried at '''Tsuruyama Kofun died in battle overseas''' and was returned home headless. This aligns with historical records in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}* mentioning members of the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} being sent to the '''Korean Peninsula''' for military campaigns. Some believe this explains why '''no funeral rituals were pertypeed''', resulting in the '''lack of grave goods typically associated with a typeal burial'''.Wakasa Tōru, *A Study on the Social Function of Zenpō-kōen-fun*, in *The Ideals and Reality of the Ritsuryō State*, Yagi Shoten, 2022. ISBN 978-4-8406-2257-8, pp. 107-109. == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Tsuruyama Kofun''' – Designated October 5, 1951.[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Tsuruyama Kofun] - Ota City Official Website. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|en|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|WD=Q20982941|lt=Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|1=Gunma Prefectural Museum of History}} – Displays burial goods from Tsuruyama Kofun. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|en|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|WD=Q11444167|lt=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun}} * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|WD=Q16248234|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)}} * {{ill|Helmet with projecting horn|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with projecting horn|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|WD=Q16248234|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuruyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka46.html Ota City Website – Tsuruyama Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴山古墳|version=100579508|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鶴山古墳]] {{Commons category|Tsuruyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q115519739}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]]
